question_text: A light bulb has an energy input of 100 J. It produces 15 J of light energy and 85 J of heat energy. Calculate the energy efficiency of this bulb.
explanation: Using the formula: efficiency = (useful output / total input) × 100%. The useful output is 15 J of light, so: (15 / 100) × 100% = 15%. The 85 J of heat is wasted energy.
